articles.title: AI can push your Stream Deck buttons for you
articles.summary: Elgato's Stream Deck 7.4 update adds Model Context Protocol support, allowing AI assistants like Claude and ChatGPT to control Stream Deck buttons remotely through voice or text commands.

articles.title: Baidu’s robotaxis froze in traffic, creating chaos
articles.summary: Baidu's robotaxis malfunctioned in Wuhan, China, freezing on highways and trapping passengers inside vehicles, causing traffic chaos and at least one accident.

articles.title: Claude Code leak exposes a Tamagotchi-style ‘pet’ and an always-on agent
articles.summary: Claude Code's 2.1.88 update accidentally exposed over 512,000 lines of TypeScript source code through a leaked source map file, revealing inner workings including unreleased features like a Tamagotchi-style pet and always-on agent capabilities.

articles.title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
articles.summary: Anthropic accidentally issued thousands of DMCA takedown notices targeting GitHub repositories containing its leaked source code. The company later retracted most notices, calling the mass action unintentional.

articles.title: Meta’s natural gas binge could power South Dakota
articles.summary: Meta's Hyperion AI data center will be powered by 10 new natural gas plants, significantly increasing energy consumption in South Dakota.

articles.title: Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
articles.summary: Cognichip raised $60M to use AI for designing AI chips, claiming to reduce development costs by 75% and cut timelines in half.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The startup capitalizes on developer demand for simpler, cost-effective alternatives to legacy platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has sparked developer backlash, with free open-source alternative Goose offering identical functionality without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative stunt decoded to a puzzle that attracted thousands of engineers and successfully hired talent.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: Quantum computers require significantly fewer resources than previously thought to break elliptic curve encryption. Recent research shows utility-scale quantum computers could crack 256-bit ECC in 10 days with 100 times less overhead.

articles.title: Google bumps up Q Day deadline to 2029, far sooner than previously thought
articles.summary: Google accelerated its Q Day deadline to 2029, when quantum computers could break current encryption. The company urges global adoption of post-quantum cryptography to protect sensitive data.

articles.title: Self-propagating malware poisons open source software and wipes Iran-based machines
articles.summary: TeamPCP, a hacking group, spreads self-propagating malware targeting unsecured cloud platforms for data theft, ransomware, and cryptocurrency mining. The group also deploys a data wiper specifically targeting Iranian machines.

articles.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from geopolitical conflict threaten plastic supply chains, as petrochemical production costs increase. Americans may face higher plastic prices due to supply chain disruptions.

articles.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices due to Middle East tensions are impacting the global economy, with plastic production facing supply chain disruptions as petrochemicals become scarcer and more expensive.

articles.title: The Download: gig workers training humanoids, and better AI benchmarks
articles.summary: Gig workers globally are training humanoid robots by recording themselves performing daily tasks via their smartphones for companies like Micro1, which sells this video data to robotics firms developing humanoids.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 advances AI computer use capabilities, breaking new frontiers in how artificial intelligence can interact with and control digital systems.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Falcon Perception appears to be an AI system or technology. Without specific article content provided, unable to generate accurate summary.

articles.title: Granite 4.0 3B Vision: Compact Multimodal Intelligence for Enterprise Documents
articles.summary: IBM released Granite 4.0 3B Vision, a compact multimodal AI model designed for enterprise document processing. It combines vision and language capabilities in a small, efficient package suitable for business applications.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft releases its 'farm of the future' toolkit as open source, enabling developers to build AI-powered agricultural solutions for sustainable farming practices.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map for forest protection. This tool aims to monitor and preserve Brazil's forests using advanced satellite technology.

articles.title: The latest AI news we announced in March 2026
articles.summary: Google announced its latest AI updates in March 2026, featuring new capabilities and developments in artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: Build with Veo 3.1 Lite, our most cost-effective video generation model
articles.summary: Google launches Veo 3.1 Lite, a cost-effective video generation model available via Gemini API and Google AI Studio for paid preview and testing.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live improves voice AI with enhanced precision and reduced latency, enabling more natural and fluid audio interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches AI manipulation risks in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to protect people from harmful exploitation.

articles.title: Lyria 3 Pro: Create longer tracks in more
articles.summary: Google introduces Lyria 3 Pro, enabling longer music track generation with improved structural understanding. The technology expands across more Google products and platforms.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, while discussions covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, a generative AI technology that enhances video game graphics in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene details and lighting to generate photorealistic visuals beyond traditional upscaling methods.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast episode 237 discusses major AI news including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Autonomous AI systems depend on data governance
articles.summary: As AI systems become more autonomous, data governance is critical to their safety and reliability. Poor data quality can make autonomous systems unpredictable, creating compliance and customer-facing risks.

articles.title: Experian uncovers fraud paradox in financial services’ AI adoption
articles.summary: Financial institutions using AI for fraud prevention face a paradox as the same technology is weaponized against them. Fraud losses exceeded $12.5 billion in 2024, with 60% of companies reporting increased losses in 2025.

articles.title: KPMG: Inside the AI agent playbook driving enterprise margin gains
articles.summary: KPMG reports enterprises plan $186M average AI spending but only 11% successfully deploy AI agents for enterprise-wide outcomes. While 64% see meaningful results, most struggle bridging incremental gains to significant margin improvements.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture enabling autonomous robots to navigate complex indoor environments by addressing localization, navigation, and path planning challenges.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, addressing a major limitation in AI models.

articles.title: Multi-level AI prompt engineering: A new tool for scientific discovery
articles.summary: Multi-level prompt engineering combined with parabolic extrapolation enables LLMs to function as theoretical collaborators, producing testable multiverse models for scientific discovery.

articles.title: IBM Releases Granite 4.0 3B Vision: A New Vision Language Model for Enterprise Grade Document Data Extraction
articles.summary: IBM released Granite 4.0 3B Vision, a specialized vision-language model for enterprise document data extraction. It uses a modular LoRA adapter architecture focused on structured data accuracy over general image captioning.

articles.title: How to Build Production Ready AgentScope Workflows with ReAct Agents, Custom Tools, Multi-Agent Debate, Structured Output and Concurrent Pipelines
articles.summary: Tutorial on building production-ready AgentScope workflows using ReAct agents, custom tools, multi-agent debate, and concurrent pipelines with structured outputs via Pydantic.

articles.title: Z.ai Launches GLM-5V-Turbo: A Native Multimodal Vision Coding Model Optimized for OpenClaw and High-Capacity Agentic Engineering Workflows Everywhere
articles.summary: Zhipu AI launched GLM-5V-Turbo, a multimodal vision coding model that bridges visual perception and code execution through native multimodal fusion, optimized for agentic workflows and complex engineering tasks.

articles.title: Still running iOS 18? Install this critical update ASAP
articles.summary: Apple released a critical iOS 18 patch protecting against the DarkSword exploit. iPhone users still on iOS 18 should install the update immediately for security.

articles.title: I tried to destroy this AirTag alternative, but it wouldn't crack - unlike others
articles.summary: Ugreen Finder Pro tags prove exceptionally durable compared to other tracking alternatives. The device withstood destruction attempts that damaged competitors' products.

articles.title: We still highly recommend these 3 older laptop models - especially while they're on sale
articles.summary: Amazon's Spring Sale has ended, but older laptop models remain available at discounted prices. These high-performing devices offer excellent value before deals expire.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are evolving from backend dashboards to more visible applications. Key trends to watch in 2026 include advancements in AI integration and deployment strategies.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: The article discusses state-managed interruptions in agentic AI systems, where human oversight pauses agent execution pipelines to maintain control and safety in autonomous operations.

articles.title: From Prompt to Prediction: Understanding Prefill, Decode, and the KV Cache in LLMs
articles.summary: Article explains LLM inference phases: prefill processes input prompts, decode generates predictions token-by-token, and KV cache optimizes decode efficiency by caching key-value pairs.

articles.title: Quantum Simulations with Python
articles.summary: Learn how to run quantum experiments using Qiskit-Aer, a Python-based quantum simulation framework that enables researchers to test quantum algorithms and circuits.

articles.title: The Inversion Error: Why Safe AGI Requires an Enactive Floor and State-Space Reversibility
articles.summary: This article examines structural design flaws in AI systems causing hallucination and corrigibility issues, arguing that safe AGI requires an enactive floor and state-space reversibility that scaling alone cannot achieve.

articles.title: How Can A Model 10,000× Smaller Outsmart ChatGPT?
articles.summary: Smaller AI models can outperform larger ones like ChatGPT through extended reasoning and computational efficiency rather than raw parameter size.

articles.title: Efficiency at Scale: NVIDIA, Energy Leaders Accelerating Power‑Flexible AI Factories to Fortify the Grid
articles.summary: NVIDIA and Emerald AI unveiled flexible AI factories that act as intelligent grid assets, enabling efficient power management and faster grid integration while improving system reliability.

articles.title: Into the Omniverse: NVIDIA GTC Showcases Virtual Worlds Powering the Physical AI Era
articles.summary: NVIDIA GTC highlighted virtual worlds driving the physical AI era, showcasing advances in OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse for transforming developer and enterprise workflows.

articles.title: Game On: Five New Titles Now Streaming on GeForce NOW
articles.summary: Five new games are now available on GeForce NOW cloud gaming platform, including retro-racing title Screamer and Honkai: Star Rail Version 4.1, allowing players to stream games instantly across devices.

articles.title: Automating competitive price intelligence with Amazon Nova Act
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Act automates competitive price monitoring for ecommerce teams, replacing manual tracking with intelligent agents that extract real-time market data, enabling faster pricing decisions and reducing operational costs.

articles.title: Build reliable AI agents with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Evaluations
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Evaluations addresses the gap between AI agent performance in demos versus production. LLM non-determinism requires systematic testing across multiple runs to understand actual behavior patterns.

articles.title: Build a FinOps agent using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Learn to build a FinOps agent using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore that consolidates AWS cost data from multiple sources, enabling finance teams to manage spending across accounts through conversational queries.

articles.title: CUDA Tile Programming Now Available for BASIC!
articles.summary: CUDA 13.1 introduced CUDA Tile, a tile-based GPU programming paradigm enabling fine-grained parallelism across multiple programming languages for broader accessibility.

articles.title: NVIDIA Extreme Co-Design Delivers New MLPerf Inference Records
articles.summary: NVIDIA's extreme co-design approach combining hardware, software, and models achieves new MLPerf inference records, focusing on real-world token output and AI factory throughput rather than peak specifications.

articles.title: Accelerate Token Production in AI Factories Using Unified Services and Real-Time AI
articles.summary: AI factories must maximize GPU utilization and token production efficiency, as even small performance drops significantly impact economic outcomes. Unified services and real-time AI monitoring are essential for preventing power waste and congestion.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint while making solutions publicly available.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough advancements in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to understand how Large Language Models make decisions through feature attribution and interaction analysis at scale. This interpretability work aims to make AI systems more transparent and trustworthy.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents information-driven methods for designing imaging systems that optimize how well measurements distinguish objects. The approach uses encoders and noise models to quantify measurement quality.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks without TD learning's limitations.
